key_themes: AI memory supercycle (HBM, DRAM, NAND) driving explosive demand
Record Q2 FY2026 earnings beat with $23.86B revenue and 75% gross margins
Sold-out HBM capacity, multi-year contracts, and strong pricing power
Compelling valuation with low single-digit forward P/E despite growth
Pullbacks viewed as buy-the-dip opportunities amid rerating to AI infrastructure name
summary: Micron Technology reported exceptional fiscal Q2 2026 results with revenue surging to $23.86 billion (nearly 3x YoY), record margins near 75%, and very strong guidance, powered by insatiable AI demand for its memory products.[[1]](https://investors.micron.com/news-releases/news-release-details/micron-technology-inc-reports-results-second-quarter-fiscal-2026)[[2]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/micron-mu-q2-earnings-report-2026.html) Investors and analysts are highly bullish, highlighting sold-out 2026 HBM capacity, multi-year contracts, pricing power, and a structural shift away from pure cyclicality toward AI infrastructure. While capex remains elevated and some volatility persists, the consensus sees MU as undervalued with street-high targets above $800 and widespread “buy the dip” sentiment.
sources: 10+ recent news articles and earnings reports (Yahoo Finance, CNBC, Seeking Alpha, MarketWatch, company IR), 25+ X posts analyzed via semantic and keyword searches (predominantly bullish investor views), Sentiment trackers (AltIndex ~79/100 positive)
